title: Who’s News: Strategic Appointments at Pasqal, PsiQuantum, and Haiqu - Quantum Computing Report
gist: A personnel roundup: Pasqal, PsiQuantum, and Haiqu each made senior commercial/technical leadership hires in mid-July 2026, with several executives arriving from adjacent enterprise-tech firms (HPE, Western Digital, Synopsys) rather than pure-play quantum backgrounds.

insights.claim: Pasqal hired Mark Armstrong, formerly HPE's EMEA Vice President and General Manager for High Performance Computing and AI, as Chief Commercial Officer for EMEA and APAC to run commercial strategy, enterprise sales, and customer expansion across energy, financial services, and advanced materials sectors.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Signals Pasqal is prioritizing enterprise go-to-market in energy, financial services, and materials verticals ahead of/alongside its EMEA-APAC commercial push; watch for named customer wins in those sectors.
insights.quote: Armstrong joins from Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), where he served as EMEA Vice President and General Manager for High Performance Computing and AI.
insights.locator: Pasqal has appointed Mark Armstrong as Chief Commercial Officer

insights.claim: PsiQuantum converted Victor Peng from interim CEO (appointed February 2026) to permanent CEO.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Removing the 'interim' qualifier suggests PsiQuantum's board has settled on leadership stability ahead of any near-term fundraising, IPO, or major deployment milestones.
insights.quote: Victor Peng has been officially named Chief Executive Officer, following his interim appointment to the role in February.
insights.locator: PsiQuantum has expanded its executive leadership team

insights.claim: PsiQuantum hired Robert Soderbery, previously Executive Vice President and General Manager of Flash Business at Western Digital, as EVP to manage engineering and operational deployment of its silicon photonics platform for advanced computing and AI networking applications.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A semiconductor-manufacturing operator (not a quantum-native hire) is being brought in to scale silicon photonics production, implying PsiQuantum is entering a manufacturing-scale-up phase rather than pure R&D.
insights.quote: Robert Soderbery has joined the company as Executive Vice President to manage the engineering and operational deployment of its silicon photonics platform across advanced computing and AI networking applications.
insights.locator: Concurrently, Robert Soderbery has joined the company

insights.claim: PsiQuantum appointed Sriram Sitaraman, formerly Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer at Synopsys, as Chief Information Officer to direct global IT strategy, security, and digital transformation.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Bringing in an EDA-industry CIO points to PsiQuantum institutionalizing enterprise-grade IT/security infrastructure as it scales toward commercial operations.
insights.quote: Sriram Sitaraman, former Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer at Synopsys, has been appointed as Chief Information Officer

insights.claim: Haiqu appointed Denise Ruffner, who previously held executive roles at IBM Quantum, Cambridge Quantum Computing (now Quantinuum), IonQ, and Atom Computing, as VP of Business Development and Commercial Operations Worldwide to drive adoption of Haiqu's newly launched Agentic Operating System, a hardware-agnostic software framework for managing and optimizing quantum R&D applications.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Haiqu is staffing up commercial adoption for a hardware-agnostic quantum software layer just after launch, a segment to watch for cross-hardware-vendor partnerships and early enterprise pilots.
insights.quote: She will lead global business development and market adoption strategies for Haiqu’s newly launched Agentic Operating System, a hardware-agnostic software framework designed to manage and optimize quantum R&D applications.

insights.claim: Across all three appointments (Pasqal's CCO from HPE, PsiQuantum's EVP from Western Digital, PsiQuantum's CIO from Synopsys), quantum computing companies are recruiting senior commercial and operational executives from adjacent large-scale semiconductor and enterprise-IT firms rather than from within the quantum industry.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: This hiring pattern suggests quantum firms perceive themselves as entering a scale-up/commercialization phase requiring proven enterprise operators, not just quantum scientists — a leading indicator to watch alongside funding and deployment announcements.
insights.locator: cross-article pattern across Pasqal and PsiQuantum appointments

insights.claim: Denise Ruffner's move to Haiqu after roles at IBM Quantum, Cambridge Quantum Computing/Quantinuum, IonQ, and Atom Computing illustrates a small, recirculating pool of commercial talent moving between competing quantum hardware and software vendors.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A shallow bench of experienced quantum commercial leaders means executive moves like this can be a useful proxy signal for which companies insiders view as ascendant.
insights.quote: Ruffner is a commercial strategist with prior executive experience at IBM Quantum, Cambridge Quantum Computing (now Quantinuum), IonQ, and Atom Computing.
